arguments.task: Open the provided GitHub Docs URL and inspect official first-party documentation pages needed to determine these fields for GitHub as an app/toolkit: one-line description, all documented auth methods for developer/API access, access tier/self-serve vs gated, API surface type (REST, GraphQL, or both), API breadth, whether an official or existing MCP exists, buildability verdict, rate limit notes, and notable signals. Prefer canonical docs.github.com, github.com, and github.github.com pages. Do not rely on search-result snippets. Read the page content and navigate to the strongest official supporting pages for authentication, GraphQL, REST, rate limits, GitHub pricing if needed, and MCP server if officially documented. Return strict JSON with keys: description, auth_methods_evidence (array of {method, evidence, url}), access_tier, access_tier_evidence, api_surface, api_breadth_evidence, mcp_exists, mcp_notes, buildability_verdict_candidate, blocker_candidate, rate_limit_notes, notable_signals, evidence_urls (array), ambiguities (array).

raw_result.description: Code hosting and collaboration platform with APIs for repositories, issues, actions, packages, and other GitHub resources.
raw_result.auth_notes: GitHub’s API docs explicitly support authentication with personal access tokens, fine-grained personal access tokens, GitHub App user/installation access tokens, and OAuth app tokens. The reviewed evidence did not establish HTTP Basic or API-key auth for current API use, so those are not included.
raw_result.gating_reason: GitHub publishes self-serve Free, Team, and Enterprise plans on its pricing page, and public REST/GraphQL docs are openly accessible without partner or admin approval.

api_surface.breadth: Broad public API surface across REST and GraphQL, covering core GitHub platform areas such as repositories, issues, pull requests, actions, packages, orgs, users, apps, billing, and more.
api_surface.mcp_notes: An existing GitHub MCP server appears to exist, but the gathered evidence did not confirm an official GitHub first-party MCP page with enough certainty.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Reviewed GitHub REST rate limit docs indicate primary limits including 60 requests/hour for unauthenticated requests and 5,000 requests/hour for authenticated requests, with higher limits for some enterprise contexts and GitHub App installations.
raw_result.notable_signals: GitHub provides both REST and GraphQL APIs in mature official docs, detailed authentication guidance for multiple token types, and explicit rate-limit documentation.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The structured field mcp_exists remains uncertain from the gathered evidence because no clearly verified first-party GitHub MCP documentation page was confirmed, and one evidence URL uses an anchor fragment rather than a cleaner canonical page.
